entry.title: CISA Rewrites Federal Patching Requirements for AI Threat Era
entry.content:
The new directive gives federal agencies three days to fix the most dangerous flaws, while less severe issues can be deferred.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Chinese, N. Korean Threat Groups Build on Asia-Pacific Success
entry.content:
North Korea's gross domestic product (GDP) has grown, in part because of the nation's state-sponsored cybercrime groups, which target financial firms and other businesses.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Max-Severity Ivanti Flaw Exploited 24 Hours After Disclosure
entry.content:
Initial methods suggest attackers had likely mapped out Ivanti's asset landscape upfront and acted quickly once the exploit became public.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Phishing Attack Volume Down 20%, But Risk Still Rising
entry.content:
Hackers are valuing quality over quantity, using AI to upgrade their phishing attacks rather than multiply them.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Claude Fable 5 Doesn't Change the Mythos Security Story
entry.content:
Stay cool: Mythos 5 is an upgrade over Mythos Preview while Fable 5 is Mythos "made safe for general use," Anthropic explains.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Most CISOs Report Pressure to Bury Bad Security News
entry.content:
Executive leaders may not be saying it aloud, but business objectives and priorities don't always promote timely disclosures.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Copilot 'SearchLeak' Attack Allows 1-Click Data Theft
entry.content:
The critical, three-stage attack is now patched, but it's part of a new group of AI prompt-injection issues that use hidden URLs and other variables.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker
entry.content:
French officials say roughly 73,000 government accounts were affected, while the threat actor claims to have stolen messages and user data from the sovereign Tchap platform. The post French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems
entry.content:
The pharmaceutical giant says the attackers gained access to personal data stored on the compromised systems. The post Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges
entry.content:
Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko admitted to working on the development of a loader for the Conti gang. The post Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Chinese Hackers Target Medical, Military, and AI Research in North America
entry.content:
Google’s Threat Intelligence Group has been tracking the cyberespionage group as UNC6508 since early 2025. The post Chinese Hackers Target Medical, Military, and AI Research in North America appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Ransomware Attack Shuts Down Mills of Australia’s Second-Largest Sugar Producer
entry.content:
Mackay Sugar was targeted in a cyberattack carried out by a threat group known as The Gentlemen. The post Ransomware Attack Shuts Down Mills of Australia’s Second-Largest Sugar Producer appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cisco Patches Another SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks
entry.content:
Cisco recently became aware of the exploitation of CVE-2026-20262, a Catalyst SD-WAN Manager zero-day that allows arbitrary file write. The post Cisco Patches Another SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cybersecurity Executives Urge the Trump Administration to Ease Restrictions on Anthropic AI Models
entry.content:
A group of cybersecurity executives and experts is asking the Trump administration to lift its directive preventing the use of Anthropic’s latest artificial intelligence models by foreign nationals. The post Cybersecurity Executives Urge the Trump Administration to Ease Restrictions on Anthropic AI Models appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Atomic Arch Supply Chain Attack Hits 1,500 AUR Packages
entry.content:
Arch Linux suspended account registrations in response to the wave of malicious packages being uploaded to AUR. The post Atomic Arch Supply Chain Attack Hits 1,500 AUR Packages appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Anthropic says US government forced it to disable cybersecurity AI models
entry.content:
According to the company, the directive cited national security authorities. It appears to be the first time such authorities have been used to curtail the export of AI models rather than chips or hardware.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cyberattack on Russian tech firm Astral disrupts business, government services for week
entry.content:
According to customer complaints, the disruption affected a range of services used by businesses, leading to interruptions in cash register operations, difficulties selling certain regulated goods, loss of access to customer portals and corporate email and problems with electronic human resources document management systems and authentication using digital certificates.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Maine closes data breach portal to the public after fake reports
entry.content:
Maine is still allowing companies to report breaches, but won’t make the portal easily available to the public until after it completes an audit of its procedures to stop such incidents, according to a press release from the Maine attorney general’s office.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
entry.content:
A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
entry.content:
An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
entry.content:
Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
entry.content:
Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
entry.content:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
entry.content:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
entry.content:
A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Data Breaches Announced by Two Digestive Health Companies
entry.content:
Cyberattacks and data breaches have recently been announced by the national gastroenterology medical group Gastro Health and Spokane Digestive Disease […] The post Data Breaches Announced by Two Digestive Health Companies appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Florida Law Firm Data Breach Affects 65,000 Individuals
entry.content:
A cyberattack at the law firm GrayRobinson has affected 65,000 individuals. Data breaches have also been announced by C2N Diagnostics […] The post Florida Law Firm Data Breach Affects 65,000 Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Labcorp Agrees to $35M Settlement to Resolve AMCA Data Breach Litigation
entry.content:
A $35,000,000 settlement has been agreed to resolve a long-running class action lawsuit against Labcorp over a 2018 cybersecurity incident […] The post Labcorp Agrees to $35M Settlement to Resolve AMCA Data Breach Litigation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Business Associates Face Increased Regulatory Scrutiny as Vendor Breaches Soar
entry.content:
The healthcare industry has the highest rate of third-party data breaches out of any sector, according to the Verizon Data […] The post Business Associates Face Increased Regulatory Scrutiny as Vendor Breaches Soar appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Clinical Trial Data Stolen in Novo Nordisk Cyberattack
entry.content:
Novo Nordisk, the Danish pharmaceutical firm behind the GLP-1 weight loss drugs Ozempic and Wegovy, has experienced a cyberattack that […] The post Clinical Trial Data Stolen in Novo Nordisk Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Clinical Registry Solutions; Jason R Egbert OD PC; VNC Health Announce Data Breaches
entry.content:
Data breaches have been announced by Clinical Registry Solutions in New York, First Sight Family Vision in Washington, and VHC […] The post Clinical Registry Solutions; Jason R Egbert OD PC; VNC Health Announce Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Infinite Campus data breach affects 137,000 school staff accounts
entry.content:
The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information from more than 137,000 school staff accounts in a Salesforce data theft attack that targeted the widely used Infinite Campus K-12 student information system in March. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: New attack turned Microsoft 365 Copilot into 1-click data theft tool
entry.content:
A critical vulnerability chain dubbed SearchLeak in Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise could allow attackers to steal sensitive data from a target's mailbox, OneDrive, or SharePoint account through a specially crafted URL. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Chinese hackers breach REDCap servers, steal medical research
entry.content:
A China-linked espionage campaign targeted exposed REDCap servers to deploy the InfiniteRed malware and steal sensitive data from a medical institution in North America. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Council of Europe investigates ShinyHunters data breach claims
entry.content:
The Council of Europe, the continent's oldest intergovernmental body, is probing claims of a data breach made by the ShinyHunters extortion group over the weekend. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Cisco fixes SD-WAN vManage flaw exploited in zero-day attacks
entry.content:
Cisco has released security updates to address a vulnerability in the Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, tracked as CVE-2026-20262, that was exploited in attacks to escalate to root privileges. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: OptinMonster WordPress plugin hacked in CDN supply-chain attack
entry.content:
WordPress plugins OptinMonster, TrustPulse, and PushEngage have been compromised in a supply-chain attack impacting Awesome Motive-s content distribution network (CDN). [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: SimpleHelp bug lets hackers create rogue remote support accounts
entry.content:
A vulnerability in the SimpleHelp remote management software allows unauthenticated attackers to create privileged technician accounts on servers using the OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication protocol. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: iRhythm discloses data breach, says hackers stole patient info
entry.content:
Digital healthcare company iRhythm Holdings has disclosed a data breach after hackers stole patients' personal and health information stored on third-party-hosted business applications. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Windows version of SprySOCKS Linux malware used to attack govt orgs
entry.content:
Windows variants for the SprySOCKS Linux malware have been used in attacks targeting government organizations in at least four countries. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Critical Fortinet FortiSandbox flaws now exploited in attacks
entry.content:
Attackers are now exploiting several critical vulnerabilities in Fortinet's FortiSandbox cyber threat detection platform, according to threat intelligence company Defused. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Ransomware gang abuses Microsoft Teams relays to hide malicious traffic
entry.content:
DragonForce ransomware used a custom malware named 'Backdoor.Turn' to hide command-and-control traffic inside Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: CISA warns of another cPanel plugin flaw exploited in attacks
entry.content:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has given U.S. government agencies three days to secure their servers against an actively exploited vulnerability (CVE-2026-54420) in the LiteSpeed cPanel user-end plugin. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: ShinyHunters Targets Education Sector with Oracle PeopleSoft Exploit
entry.content:
From Mandiant and Google Threat Intelligence Group, an advisory: Mandiant and Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) have identified an active compromise and extortion campaign attributed to UNC6240 (ShinyHunters) targeting Oracle PeopleSoft application infrastructure. The activity was observed between May 27, 2026, and June 9, 2026 and is consistent with the exploitation of CVE-2026-35273, a critical remote... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Labcorp reaches $35M settlement over American Medical Collection Agency breach
entry.content:
Do you remember the horrific American Medical Collection Agency (Retrieval-Masters Creditors Bureau Inc.) breach in 2019? You can refresh your memory by scrolling through the Related posts below this one, but TL;DR: LabCorp was one of AMCA’s clients that was affected by the breach, and in July 2019, they notified HHS that 10,251,784 patients had... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation
entry.content:
Lawrence Abrams reports: A Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland to the United States last year has pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges tied to the Conti ransomware operation. The U.S. Department of Justice announced Thursday that 44-year-old Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit wire fraud for his role in Conti ransomware attacks conducted... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: South Korea Hands Coupang a Record-Breaking $409 Million Data Privacy Fine
entry.content:
DataBreaches has been impressed by South Korea’s response to data breaches ever since reading about how its financial regulator responded to three credit card companies whose customers suffered a major data leak. Unlike any enforcement action DataBreaches had ever seen levied here in the U.S., the firms had their ability to enroll new customers suspended... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: ShinyHunters Claims Theft of 297GB of Council of Europe Data; Claims Unconfirmed As Yet
entry.content:
Bhaswati Guha Majumder reports: The cybercrime group ShinyHunters has claimed responsibility for a major breach involving the Council of Europe, threatening to publish hundreds of gigabytes of allegedly stolen data unless its demands are met by 16 June. The claim comes in the wake of a confirmed cybersecurity incident affecting European infrastructure. According to information... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Novo Nordisk reports data breach, tells clinical trial patients to ‘remain vigilant’
entry.content:
Eric Sagonowsky reports: As cybersecurity threats have proliferated across industries in recent years, biopharma companies have emerged as prominent targets, with intellectual property, patient data and other sensitive information at stake. Now, Novo Nordisk is the latest drug giant to report a data breach. In a Thursday incident notice, Novo said it recently identified a security... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: UK: Hotel guests issued urgent ‘check’ alert as personal details stolen from major chain
entry.content:
Elaine Blackburne reports: Hotel guests have been warned to stay alert for convincing fraudulent messages following a data breach at a major hotel chain. Personal information belonging to individuals with reservations at one of the chain’s properties was compromised over a six-month period. BWH Hotels, the parent company behind WorldHotels, Best Western Hotels & Resorts,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: AU: American Express ordered to fix security gaps after customer was spied on
entry.content:
Harriet Alexander and Julie Lewis report: The privacy watchdog has ordered American Express to rectify security flaws in five of its data systems to guard against “insider threats” and to restrict employee access to specific customer information to protect vulnerable and high-profile customers. Privacy Commissioner Carly Kind found the payments giant had “failed to implement... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: JLR ordered 30,000 staff to reset passwords in person after cyberattack
entry.content:
Aimee Turner reports: Jaguar Land Rover ordered all 30,000 employees to reset their passwords in person following a cyberattack that raised concerns staff credentials had been compromised. Speaking at Infosecurity Europe, former Jaguar Land Rover chief information security officer Ashish Shrestha revealed the company required employees to physically verify their identity before resetting passwords after... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Scoop: FulcrumSec Leaks Novo Nordisk Data After $25M Demand Goes Unpaid
entry.content:
Danish pharma giant Novo Nordisk disclosed a cybersecurity incident last week, and although the firm’s name may not be familiar to everyone, they are a major producer of insulin and semaglutide. Semaglutide is marketed as Wegovy for weight loss and Ozempic for Type 2 diabetes. In its June 11 update, the firm stated that the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Unknown
entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

BreachForums Report Victim breachforu.ms Threat Actor ShadowByt3$ Date Discovered Jun 15, 2026 Description BreachForums is an online discussion forum associated with â¦June 15, 2026 reported a ransomware. Attributed to LockBit.

entry.title: Ultrahuman Healthcare Private Limited
entry.content:
